questions.text:
Match the correct pair:
a. Unhaalyaat bharpoor paani pyaave - I. Sthalavaachak Kriyaavisheshan
b. Jase karaave tase bharaave - II. Kaalavaachak Kriyaavisheshan
k. Hallee mahagaai zhaali aahe - III. Reeteevaachak Kriyaavisheshan
d. Pudhe patangan aahe - IV. Parinamavaachak Kriyaavisheshan
Jya vyanjananchaa uchchaar kartaanaa havaa adhik pramaanaat vaaparali jaate tya vyanjanannaa kaay mhanataat?
'Ik' pratyayaant shabd hrasva astataat. Ya niyamaachaa aadhaar gheun pudheel shabdaateel shuddha 'shabd' olakha.
'Aajanma' ya saamasik shabdaachaa samaas olakhaa.
(Passage-based) Maanavi svabhaavache mool svaroop samajnyaasaathi aavashyak aslele ghatak konte?
options: Dnyaanche utkhanan, sanshodhan va manan hone
Maanavi manaache bhaavtarang samajoon denaaree shakti

questions.text: (Passage-based) Maanavaachya shodhak buddhishi sambandhit aslelaa kamaal maryadaa konate?
(Passage-based) 'Vaidnyanikdrushtya zaalele maanavi sanshodhan apoornn aahe' ase Shreeramakrishnaanna kaa vaate?
options: Kaaran mukhya svabhaavache marma va jadanghadana ukalnyaat manushya asamarth tharla aahe mhanoon
Manushya svabhaav mhanje jaldheesthali asnaare mrugajal aahe mhanoon

questions.text: (Passage-based) Maanavi svabhaavaroopi mrugajalaachya burkhyaat laplelaa saamaajik gahan prashna kontaa?
options: Manushya manah patalaavar umatat jaanaare berevaaeet bhaavtarang
Samaajaateel daambhik, khotarade, bhondu svabhaavaachi maanase olakhayachi kashi?

questions.text: (Passage-based) Aaple man-svabhaav-vichaar paripakva karnaari Shreeramakrishnanna abhipret aslelaa shakti kontee?
'Hatabal' yaa shabdache vibhakti pratyayaachya sahaayyaane honare panchamee bahuvreehee samaasaache roop konte?
'Malaa pareekshet pahilaa varg milhaavaa.' Yaa vaakyaachaa prakaar olakhaa.
Var, khaalee, pudhe hee kriyaavisheshan avyaye jevhaa svatantra na yetaa shabdaalaa jodoon yetaat tevhaa tyaannaa kaay mhatale jaate?
'Udyaapaasoon bus va railwesevaa suroo hoeel.' Yaa vaakyaachaa rachanenoosaar prakaar olakhaa.

Shreemant vhaave ase konaala vaatat naahi?
Yaa vaakyaache vidhaanaarthee vaakyaat roopantar karaa.
a. Gareeb vhaave ase malaa vaatate.
b. Shreemant vhaave ase malaa ajibaat vaatat naahi.
c. Shreemant vhaave ase sarvaanna vaatate.
d. Gareeb vhaave ase anekanna vaatate.

Khaaleelaiki 'Kanadi' shabdaanchaa gat olakhaa.
a. Arj, hukoom
b. Bhaakari, toop
c. Pagaar, tambaakhoo
d. Tulaal, ghee

Choose the correctly spelt word/s.
a. PROMENADE
b. PROMINANCE
c. PROMISCUOUS

Match the following:
a. The feeling that you have previously experienced something - I. De jure
b. The crime of killing one's brother or sister - II. Regicide
c. According to the law - III. Fratricide
d. The crime of killing a king or queen - IV. Déjà vu

She _______ four awards.
Choose the correct alternative to complete the sentence meaningfully.

Identify complex sentence/s.
a. I know that Mohan is a bank manager.
b. What you told me is true.
c. I helped him because he was very weak.

In which of the following is an article used correctly?
a. I was tired of the life.
b. The both anxious parents sat up the half night.
c. The Kapoors are our neighbours.

(Passage on Diet & Nutrition) The passage focuses on:
a. Nutrition for vigour and vitality.
b. Importance of nutrients in curing diseases.
c. Types of nutrients.
d. Balanced diet.

(Passage on Diet & Nutrition) What are the causes of nutritional deficiencies?
a. Use of preservatives in food
b. Intake of refined food
c. No use of pesticides and chemical fertilizers
d. Use of wholesome foods and fruits
e. Long interval between harvesting and consumption of vegetables and fruits.

(Passage on Diet & Nutrition) The phrase 'well balanced diet' refers to:
a. Intake of a variety of foods.
b. Consumption of whole meal bread and the white stuff.
c. Proper consumption of vegetables and fruits.
d. Intake of the foods which supply all the essential nutrients.
(Passage on Diet & Nutrition) Find out the word in the passage which means 'Not supplied in required quantity'

a. Absence of any of the essential nutrients may lead to death.
b. Oxygen and water are vital elements in human cells.
c. Diseases caused by nutritional deficiencies are irreparable.
d. Diet is one of the essential factors in building and maintaining good health.
Choose the correct alternative/s.

I am extremely happy.
Select the correct alternative to tag question to the above sentence.

Before Akshay came to Bollywood, he _______ in hotels for five years.
Select the correct alternative to complete the sentence meaningfully.

Choose the alternative closest in meaning to the word in capitals.
The negotiations have reached an IMPASSE.
options: Point at which further development is blocked.
Point at which highest profit is guaranteed.
Point of no profit no loss.
Point at which a meeting concludes.

questions.text:
Identify the part of speech of the word underlined in the following sentence:
Don't go near the edge.

Which of the following is not a synonym of the word GARRULOUS?
a. Loquacious
b. Talkative
c. Gauge

Which of the following features of the Indian Constitution are inconsistent with the Federal principle?
a. One Constitution for the States
b. Single citizenship
c. More legislative power to the Centre
d. Appointment of Governor by the Centre
e. States field of legislation cannot be invaded by the Centre

Consider the following statements:
a. 'Fundamental Duties' is a salient feature of the Indian Constitution.
b. These were added by the 44th Constitutional Amendment Act in the Constitution.
options: a is correct, b is wrong
Both a and b are correct
Both a and b are wrong

questions.text: Which Article of the Constitution provides for the Finance Commission?

What among the following is not true about the Public Accounts Committee?
a. The Committee was set up in 1921.
b. Its main function is to examine the annual reports of CAG.
c. It consists of 15 Lok Sabha members and 7 Rajya Sabha members.
d. The term of the office of the Public Accounts Committee is one year.

Which of the following statements are correct about Lok Sabha sessions?
a. Lok Sabha shall meet at least twice in a year.
b. Interval between two consecutive sessions shall be less than six months.
c. The President summons the Lok Sabha to meet.
d. Speaker of Lok Sabha will decide time and place of Lok Sabha session.

Consider the following statements and choose the correct option.
a. A Constitutional Amendment Bill can be introduced in the Parliament only.
b. The President must give assent to the Constitutional Amendment Bill after it is duly passed by the Parliament and ratified by State Legislatures, where necessary.
c. Constitutional Amendment process is laid down in Article 365.

The Chairman of National Human Rights Commission is appointed by the President on the recommendation of a Committee of certain persons. Who among the following is/are not member of that Committee?
a. The Prime Minister
b. The Home Minister
c. The Leader of the Opposition Party in the Lok Sabha
d. The Chairman of Rajya Sabha
Which of the following is an exclusive power of Rajya Sabha?
options: Setting up of All India Services
Electoral power for Vice President's Election
Power related to introduce the Finance bill

questions.text: 'To provide opportunities for education by Mother-Father or Guardian to their child or ward between the age of 6 and 14 years.' By which Constitutional Amendment Act was this Fundamental Duty added in the Constitution?

Arrange the following Committees according to their establishment year in ascending order:
a. Ashok Mehta Committee
b. Balwant Rai Mehta Committee
c. G.V.K. Rao Committee
d. K. Santhanam Committee

Two wires A and B are made of same material and having their lengths in the ratio 6:1 are connected in series. The P.D. across the wires are 3 V and 2 V respectively. Find RB/RA, where RA and RB are the radii of the 2 wires.
Sound waves are longitudinal mechanical waves that can travel through which of the following mediums?
The focal lengths of two lenses are 2 m and 1 m, then the total power of a combination of these lenses is
The chemical reagent used to remove permanent hardness of water is/are
The resultant dipole moment in NF3 molecule is _______ and in the direction of _______ atom/s.
Which of the following set of quantum numbers is not possible?
questions.explanation: This question was cancelled by the examination authority.

questions.text: Over which of the following latitudes is the Geostationary satellite set?
In interpreting the aerial photograph, the pair of adjacent aerial photographs is called
State which of the following statements is true?
options: In the raster data model, the basic building block is the individual cell.
In the vector data model, the basic building block is the individual cell.
In the vector data model, well-defined boundaries are represented.
In the vector data model, locations are well represented.
questions.explanation: This question was cancelled by the examination authority.

questions.text: What is the long form of the term 'HTML', used in relation to Internet?
The emergence of electronic commerce can be defined as
options: The transaction of business on the Internet

questions.text: What is the full form of ASCII?
options: All Standard Characters for Information Identity
American Standard Code for Information Interchange
All Simple Codes for Infotainment Interests
American Standard Characters for Information Interchange

questions.text: What is cyber bullying?
In the Western Himalayas, fruit gardens are developed due to
options: Soil in the Western Himalayan region being good for the growth of fruit gardens
the Western Himalayan region receiving rainfall in winter, which is favourable for the growth of fruit gardens
the amount of rainfall being generally low in the Western Himalayas
Mountain climate being favourable for the growth of fruit gardens

questions.text: Which of the following atmospheric conditions is responsible for the occurrence of the Indian climatic phenomena 'Retreating Monsoon'?
options: Expansion of high pressure belt from north-west to south-east direction in India.
The south-west monsoon winds reach the foothills of the Himalayas, blocked by the Himalayan ranges, and then retreat from the Himalayas.
Occurrence of steep pressure gradient in the Indian sub-continent.

questions.text: Arrange in correct order the following mountain ranges from the South to North.
options: Shivalik range, Greater Himalayas, Zaskar range, Pirpanjal range, Ladakh range, Karakoram range
Shivalik range, Pirpanjal range, Greater Himalayas, Zaskar range, Ladakh range, Karakoram range
Shivalik range, Zaskar range, Pirpanjal range, Ladakh range, Greater Himalayas, Karakoram range
Shivalik range, Pirpanjal range, Zaskar range, Ladakh range, Karakoram range, Greater Himalayas

questions.text:
The periodical difference between the shortest day and the longest day in India is given below:
a. In south near Kanyakumari it is about 45 minutes.
b. In north near Leh in Ladakh it is about 4 hours.
Why does this time difference take place?
options: Kanyakumari is situated near the equator and Leh is far away from the equator.
Location of Kanyakumari is in the tropical hemisphere while Leh is located in the temperate hemisphere.
The periodical difference between the day time and night time is minimum in South India while it goes on increasing towards North India.
The axis of the Earth is inclined to the plane of the ecliptic at an angle of 66° 30' giving rise to increasing length of day and night from the equator to the poles.

questions.text: Which of the following types of forests is found in areas above 1200 m altitude and receiving 400 cm and more amount of rainfall in the Sahyadris?

Given below are two statements, one labelled as Assertion (A) and the other as Reason (B).
(A) Groundwater resource has been depleted intensively in Maharashtra.
(B) There is a need for groundwater conservation in Maharashtra.
options: Both (A) and (B) are true and (B) is the correct explanation of (A)
Both (A) and (B) are true but (B) is not the correct explanation of (A)

questions.text: Why is the sex-ratio in the districts of Sindhudurg and Ratnagiri in Konkan maximum in Maharashtra?
options: Low rate of female foeticide in these districts of the Konkan
Out-migration of male working population from these districts of Konkan to the industrial region of Mumbai
Dowry is given to bride on the occasion of marriage
The number of female children is increasing due to improvement in health and medical facilities in these districts

questions.text: A woman says, 'If you reverse my own age, the figures represent my father's age. He is of course older to me and the difference between our ages is three-eleventh of their sum.' The women's age is
There are 25 people in a group. If all shake hands with one another, how many handshakes are possible?

P, Q, R, S, T, U and V are travelling in three different vehicles. There are at least two passengers in each vehicle (Car, Rickshaw, Lorry) and each vehicle has passengers of both the sexes. There are two Merchants, two Doctors and three Lawyers among them. R is a lady doctor and she does not travel with the pair of sisters, P and U. Q, a male merchant, travels with only V, a Lawyer in vehicle car. S is a male doctor. Two persons belonging to same profession do not travel in the same vehicle. P is not a merchant and travels in vehicle Rickshaw.
In which vehicle does R travel?
What will be the mirror image of a clock at 3:37?
Observe the following diagrams (A), (B), (C) - a rectangle 9x4, a parallelogram 6x6, and a triangle with base 8 and height 9.
options: The area of all diagrams are different.
The area of all diagrams are same.
The perimeter of all diagrams are same.
Diagram (A) and (B) have the same perimeter.

questions.text: Anil, Sanjay, Sunil, Deepak, Mahesh, Prashant and Suresh are seated in a row. Sunil is seated between Deepak and Anil, Mahesh is seated between Prashant and Suresh and Sanjay is seated between Deepak and Prashant. Anil and Suresh are seated at both ends of the row, then where is Deepak seated?
options: In the middle of Sunil and Sanjay
In the middle of Anil and Sanjay
In the middle of Sanjay and Mahesh
In the middle of Sunil and Prashant

questions.text:
Which one of the following numbers will occupy the blank space?
[Grid pattern: ?, 45, 29 / 15, 17, 21]
The day after is Monday. The date tomorrow is 3. Then what will be the date on next Sunday?

Statements:
All locks are keys.
All keys are doors.
Some doors are windows.
Some windows are floors.
Conclusions:
I. Some keys are windows.
II. Some floors are not doors.
III. Any locks are not windows.
options: None of the conclusions are correct
Conclusions I and II are correct

questions.text: There are five Saturdays in a month of 30 days. What will be the first day of that month?

In the following number series a number is wrong. Select that number:
3, 7, 16, 32, 56, 93, 142

Insert the missing character.
[Grid: XY=12, 10, IP / FJ=9, 8, ? / AC=?, 11, NV]

If 'P × Q' means P is sister of Q, 'P + Q' means P is mother of Q, 'P - Q' means P is father of Q, 'P ÷ Q' means P is brother of Q, 'P = Q' means P is daughter of Q, 'P > Q' means P is husband of Q, then which of the following represents 'B is the great-grandfather of R'?

Two metro trains are running for one and a half hours in opposite directions at a same speed of 264 kmph on a circular railway-track. If the distance of the track from the center-point is 23 km, then how many times will those metro trains pass each other?
A can build a house alone in 15 days but B alone can build it in 10 days. A and B work on alternate days. If A works on the first day, in how many days will the house be built?

Find the odd-man-out in the letter group series.
XZB, DGJ, SWA, MRX, AGM

Select the correct option in place of the question mark in the given number structure.
[Diagram: 6/2=72, 9=?, 7/6=5, with center values]

Gaurav is older than Saurav but younger than Saurabh. Saurav is as old as Arnav but older than Pranav.
Which of the statements given below is/are correct?
a. Arnav is younger than Saurav.
b. Arnav is older than Saurabh.
c. Arnav is older than Pranav.
d. Arnav is older than Gaurav.

Six persons are sitting around a circular table. Anand is facing Heena, who is sitting to the left of Arvind and to the right of Sanjay. Suman is to the right of Arvind. Manoj is facing Arvind. If Heena and Manoj, Arvind and Sanjay mutually exchange their positions, who is now sitting next to the left of Manoj?

If the codes given for alphabets are repeated serially for a whole sequence of alphabets then select the codes for the given words.
A=□, B=#, C=O, D=△, E=θ
FAIR WELL = ?

While investigating about a stolen car, the police received information from different persons as follows:
a. The car number has four digits.
b. The sum of digits of the car number is divisible by 4.
c. The unit place digit of the car number is 4.
18 teams have registered their names for the cricket competition. Every team has to play a match with another team. How many total matches will there be?

Select by using the following group of letters, the letter that comes third to the right of the letter that comes immediately to the left of the letter, that comes third to the right of the letter, immediately to the left of the letter B.
A B C D E F G H

Find the missing number in the series:
43, 95, 167, ?, 373, 507

In the following English alphabet's letter groups series, which group will come in the place of the question mark (?)
BZA, DYC, FXE, ?, JVI

Match the correct pairs of Act and its Year for the environmental protection.
a. Air (Prevention and Control of Pollution) - I. 1974
b. Wild Life Protection - II. 1980
c. Water (Prevention and Control of Pollution) - III. 1981
d. Forest Conservation - IV. 1972
Kyoto Conference is concerned with:
options: developing alternatives to ozone depleting substances

questions.text: Greenhouse effect is enhanced in the environment by the _______ gas.

Match the following pairs:
a. Chipko Movement - I. Fertilizer Industry
b. Kerala Shastra Sahitya Parishad - II. Deforestation
c. Soil Conservation Movement - III. Hydro Electricity Generating Project
d. Bombay Protecting Committee - IV. Tawa River Project
Depletion of ozone layer is due to oxide of

a. Ramakrishna Paramhansa
b. Ramdas Swami
c. Aryabhatta
d. Saint Ramanujacharya
Who is the author of the book 'By Many a Happy Accident: Recollections of a Life'?

Consider the following statements:
a. Maharashtra Legislative Assembly passed 'Shakti' bill in December 2021.
b. There is a provision of death penalty to rapist.
c. There is a provision of 10 years imprisonment to acid attacker on women.
d. This bill was submitted in the Assembly by the Finance Minister.
Which of the following statements are correct?

Match the pairs:
a. Sinopharm - I. America
b. Covishield - II. Russia
c. Sputnik - III. China
d. Moderna - IV. India
Who received the Nobel Peace Prize in 2021?
In March 2021, Lok Sabha, Rajya Sabha TV were merged to form which TV?

Consider the following statements about 'Dadasaheb Phalke' Award:
a. Sponsored by the Directorate of Film Festivals.
b. It is given for outstanding contribution to the development of Indian Cinema.
c. Prize amount is Rs. 1 lakh.
d. Award for the year 2021 has been given to Rajinikanth.
Which of the above statements are correct?

questions.text: Consider the following statements about New Education Policy - 2020 (NEP - 2020). Which of the statements is not correct?
options: Its aim is to make India a global knowledge superpower.
It will replace Policy on Education 1986 (NPE - 1986).
Instead of 10 + 2 there will be 7 + 4 + 3 + 1 pattern.

questions.text:
Which of the following statements is correct about the medal tally at the Tokyo 2020 Olympics?
a. Host country Japan secured first position.
b. India secured 48th position with total seven medals.
c. India secured three Gold Medals.
